steps.title: Welcome to Day 22 — AI for Project Management
steps.body: Welcome to Week 4 — the final week of your AI journey. This week you apply AI to real-world domains: project management, finance, learning, creativity, productivity, and your career. Starting today: how AI turns project chaos into clarity.

steps.title: Project chaos — the story of Karim
steps.body: Karim runs a small marketing team. They have five projects, shifting deadlines, team members who forget updates, and a client who wants a timeline 'by end of week.' Karim spends more time tracking projects than doing the work. He needs AI to bring order to the chaos.

steps.title: What AI can do for your projects
steps.body: AI helps with every phase of a project: (1) Planning — break big goals into tasks. (2) Scheduling — estimate timelines realistically. (3) Risk spotting — identify what might go wrong. (4) Communication — draft status updates. (5) Tracking — summarize progress. You do not need expensive project software. A good AI prompt can do most of this.

steps.title: Plan a project with AI

fields.label: What project do you need to plan?
fields.placeholder: launch a new website by end of month

fields.label: What are your constraints — time, budget, people?
fields.placeholder: two weeks, three people, no new budget

fields.label: What are two or three key milestones?
fields.placeholder: content ready, design approved, final review

steps.template: Help me plan this project: Goal: {{project_goal}}. Constraints: {{constraints}}. Milestones: {{key_milestones}}. Break this into weekly tasks with owners and deadlines. Identify dependencies. Suggest how to track progress each week.

steps.title: Save your project plan
steps.body: Paste the project plan AI created for you. You will use this to guide your work.

steps.title: Spotting risks before they happen
steps.body: Most project failures are predictable — if you stop to think about them. AI is great at asking 'what could go wrong?' Prompt: 'Here is my project plan. List everything that could delay or derail it. For each risk, suggest a backup plan.' You will be amazed at what you missed.

steps.title: Risk identification check
steps.question: What is the best way to use AI for risk identification?
options: Ask AI to make decisions for you
Show AI your plan and ask it to list possible risks and solutions
Let AI run the project instead of you
feedback: AI should suggest, not decide — you stay in charge.
Correct. AI reviews your plan and spots risks you might have missed.
AI is a tool, not a replacement for your judgment.

steps.title: Deadline management with AI
steps.body: Deadlines slip when estimates are wrong. Ask AI: 'Given these tasks and my availability, create a realistic timeline. Flag any task where my estimate seems too optimistic.' AI can even suggest how to communicate delays professionally — because bad news delivered well is a skill too.

steps.title: Build your project timeline

fields.label: List 3-5 key tasks for your project
fields.placeholder: design homepage, write content, set up analytics

fields.label: What is your target completion date?
How many hours per week can you dedicate?

steps.template: Create a timeline for these tasks: {{tasks}}. Target: {{deadline}}. Capacity: {{capacity}}. Show me week-by-week what to work on. Flag any task that needs more time. Suggest a buffer for unexpected delays.

steps.title: Save your timeline
steps.body: Paste your AI-generated project timeline. Refer back when priorities shift.

steps.title: Team coordination made simple
steps.body: Coordinating a team? Use AI to: (1) Draft meeting agendas that respect everyone's time. (2) Write clear task assignments so nothing gets lost. (3) Summarize long email threads into action items. (4) Create a RACI chart — who is Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed. Prompt: 'Given these team members and their roles, help me assign these tasks clearly.'

steps.title: Progress tracking — the weekly pulse
steps.body: The best project managers run a weekly pulse: what was done, what is blocked, what is next. AI can help you run this in five minutes. Prompt: 'Here are my tasks from last week. Here is what happened. Help me write a one-paragraph status update for my stakeholders and suggest priorities for next week.'

steps.title: Your project management toolkit
items: I can plan a project by describing the goal and asking AI for tasks and timeline
I can spot risks by asking AI to review my plan
I can build a realistic timeline with AI estimating time per task
I can coordinate my team using AI for agendas and assignments
I can track progress weekly with an AI-generated status update

questions.question: What is the first step in using AI for project planning?
options: Describe your goal, constraints, and milestones to AI
Wait until the project is halfway done

questions.question: How should you use AI for risk identification?
options: Show AI your plan and ask what could go wrong and how to prevent it
Decide risks yourself without AI input

questions.question: AI can replace the need for a project manager entirely.

steps.title: Day 22 complete — Project Manager
steps.body: You can now use AI to plan projects, spot risks, manage deadlines, coordinate teams, and track progress. Tomorrow: AI for financial planning — putting your money decisions on solid ground.
